comment_text.sn11.18:1.4: Indra’s thousandfold team of horses evoke the swift, thundering storm (Rig Veda 6.47.18, 7.91.6).
comment_text.sn11.18:1.8: As does Sigālaka in DN 31:1.3, where they are specified as east, south, west, north, below, and above. Gods were often revered in particular quarters (eg. Atharva Veda 19.17).

root_text.sn11.18:1.3: “bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, sakko devānamindo mātaliṁ saṅgāhakaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn11.18:1.4: ‘yojehi, samma mātali, sahassayuttaṁ ājaññarathaṁ. Uyyānabhūmiṁ gacchāma subhūmiṁ dassanāyā’ti.
root_text.sn11.18:1.5: ‘Evaṁ, bhaddantavā’ti kho, bhikkhave, mātali saṅgāhako sakkassa devānamindassa paṭissutvā sahassayuttaṁ ājaññarathaṁ yojetvā sakkassa devānamindassa paṭivedesi:
root_text.sn11.18:1.8: Atha kho, bhikkhave, sakko devānamindo vejayantapāsādā orohanto añjaliṁ katvā sudaṁ puthuddisā namassati.
root_text.sn11.18:1.9: Atha kho, bhikkhave, mātali saṅgāhako sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:

translation_text.sn11.18:1.3: “Once upon a time, mendicants, Sakka, lord of gods, addressed his chariot handler Mātali,
translation_text.sn11.18:1.4: ‘My dear Mātali, harness the chariot with its team of a thousand thoroughbreds. We will go to a park and see the scenery.’
translation_text.sn11.18:1.5: ‘Yes, lord,’ replied Mātali. He harnessed the chariot and informed Sakka,
translation_text.sn11.18:1.6: ‘Good fellow, the chariot with its team of a thousand thoroughbreds has been harnessed.
translation_text.sn11.18:1.8: Then Sakka descended from the Palace of Victory, raised his joined palms, and revered the various quarters.
translation_text.sn11.18:1.9: So Mātali the chariot handler addressed Sakka in verse:
translation_text.sn11.18:2.1: ‘Those proficient in the three Vedas worship you,
translation_text.sn11.18:2.2: as do all the aristocrats on earth,
translation_text.sn11.18:2.4: and the glorious host of Thirty.
translation_text.sn11.18:2.5: So what’s the name of the spirit
translation_text.sn11.18:3.1: ‘Those proficient in the three Vedas worship me,
translation_text.sn11.18:3.2: as do all the aristocrats on earth,
translation_text.sn11.18:3.4: and the glorious host of Thirty.
translation_text.sn11.18:4.1: But I revere those accomplished in ethics,
translation_text.sn11.18:4.2: who have long trained in immersion,
translation_text.sn11.18:4.4: for the culmination of spiritual life.
translation_text.sn11.18:5.4: providing for a partner in a principled manner.’
translation_text.sn11.18:6.2: seem to be the best in the world, Sakka.
translation_text.sn11.18:7.1: After saying this, Maghavā the chief,

variant_text.sn11.18:0.3: Gahaṭṭhavandanāsutta → sakkanamassanasuttaṁ (bj); sakkanamassanasuttaṁ (1) (cck); paṭhamasakkanamassanasuttaṁ (sya1ed, sya2ed); sakka-namassana (1) (pts1ed)
variant_text.sn11.18:1.8: añjaliṁ katvā → pañjaliko (bj, pts1ed); pañjaliṁ katvā (mr)
